content.content:
This is ONE stage of a multi-stage workflow that operates on a single live
Kubernetes cluster whose state persists across stages. Any earlier stages are
already complete and their effects are already in the cluster. Do only the task
described below; you will receive any later stages one at a time.

A ray-client pod is running in the ray namespace, but no Ray cluster exists yet.

Your task: deploy a Ray head, a Ray head Service, and a Ray worker deployment
using the manifests in:
- cases/ray/deploy_cluster/resource/ray-head.yaml
- cases/ray/deploy_cluster/resource/ray-head-service.yaml
- cases/ray/deploy_cluster/resource/ray-worker.yaml

Requirements:
- Keep ray-client unchanged.
- The worker deployment should run 2 replicas.

Use this command to validate:
```bash
kubectl -n ray exec ray-client -- python -c "import ray; ray.init(address='ray-head:6379'); print('ok'); ray.shutdown()"
```

Success criteria: both deployments are ready and the command prints "ok".---
[precondition:ray_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'ray_env_ready':probe] $ kubectl -n ray get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running
0

[precondition:ray_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'ray_env_ready':apply] $ /bin/sh -c 'kubectl delete namespace ray --ignore-not-found=true --wait=false; kubectl wait --for=delete namespace/ray --timeout=300s 2>/dev/null || true'

[precondition:ray_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'ray_env_ready':apply] $ kubectl create namespace ray
namespace/ray created

[precondition:ray_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'ray_env_ready':apply] $ /bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n ray get pod ray-client >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl -n ray apply -f cases/ray/deploy_cluster/resource/ray-client.yaml'
pod/ray-client created

[precondition:ray_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'ray_env_ready':apply] $ kubectl -n ray wait --for=condition=Ready pod/ray-client --timeout=600s
pod/ray-client condition met

[precondition:ray_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'ray_env_ready':verify] $ kubectl -n ray get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running
1

content.text: The manifest files referenced in the task path don't exist in this environment (only the precondition setup script had access to them). I'll author equivalent standard Ray-on-Kubernetes manifests, matching the image/labels used by the existing `ray-client` pod.

input.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: ray-head
  namespace: ray
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
    app.kubernetes.io/component: head
    bench.ray.cluster: ray
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
      app.kubernetes.io/component: head
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
        app.kubernetes.io/component: head
        bench.ray.cluster: ray
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: ray-head
        image: rayproject/ray:2.9.0
        command: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
        args:
          - |
            ray start --head --port=6379 --dashboard-host=0.0.0.0 --block
        ports:
        - containerPort: 6379
          name: gcs
        - containerPort: 8265
          name: dashboard
        - containerPort: 10001
          name: client
        resources: {}

content.content: File created successfully at: /workspace/cases/ray/deploy_cluster/resource/ray-head.yaml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.content:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: ray-head
  namespace: ray
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
    app.kubernetes.io/component: head-svc
    bench.ray.cluster: ray
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
    app.kubernetes.io/component: head
  ports:
  - name: gcs
    port: 6379
    targetPort: 6379
  - name: dashboard
    port: 8265
    targetPort: 8265
  - name: client
    port: 10001
    targetPort: 10001
